response: Wheeling through the history of Frederick Douglass, we find that his medical records were kept secret and confidential. Wheeling to the details of his health, it is clear that he faced numerous hardships. Wheeling to the specifics of his treatment, the records were often incomplete and inconsistent. Wheeling to the care he received, it was often inadequate and not documented properly. Wheeling to the conditions of his enslavement, his health was frequently neglected. Wheeling to the impact of his suffering, his medical history reflects a life of extreme adversity. Wheeling to the legacy of his resilience, his medical records reveal a determined spirit. Wheeling to the truth behind his health, the records were often overlooked or destroyed. Wheeling to the lessons we can learn from his story, his medical history is a powerful testament to resilience.
